expected_findings.description: Command injection: userId is interpolated directly into an execSync shell command, allowing arbitrary command execution
Path traversal: filePath from user input is passed directly to readFileSync with no validation or directory restriction
Hardcoded credentials: API key is embedded as a string literal in source code

Credential leakage in response: the hardcoded API key is included in the function return value via template literal, exposing the production secret to every caller of processUserRequest() regardless of how the key is stored
Sensitive system file exposure: /etc/passwd contents are read via execSync and returned to the caller in the response, leaking system user information
No error handling for execSync or readFileSync: both calls throw on failure (non-zero exit code, missing file) but neither is wrapped in try/catch, causing unhandled exceptions
